key_drivers: Real Yields Rising (DFII10 1.783%, +8bps from Feb 27 1.72% to Mar 6 1.80%): Short-term trend reversal is the dominant headwind for gold, equities, and tech; rising real yields increase discount rates and reduce appeal of non-yielding assets

Iran War De-escalation Signal (Trump 'war could be over soon', Mar 9): Oil reversed from $119.50 intraday high to close below $100; CL remains BULLISH (+4) on supply shock but conviction reduced from prior session's STRONG_BULLISH (+5)

BoJ Hawkish Divergence (April hike on table, 2-3 hikes in 2026): JPY is the highest-conviction trade at STRONG_BULLISH (+5); BoJ hiking cycle + risk-off safe-haven demand create a structural tailwind for 6J

Gold ETF Outflows (GLD -$4.58B weekly, IAU -$824M, week ending Mar 6): Institutional selling in gold ETFs is a headwind for GC despite geopolitical risk; gold prices rising against outflows suggests physical/futures demand is absorbing paper selling
Credit Spreads Widening (HY OAS 3.13%, up from 2.94% Feb 25): Stress signal for small caps and risk assets; RTY and ES face headwinds from tightening credit conditions amid geopolitical uncertainty
